tasks.subject: [relay-mesh-quality-improvements] [P3] Split relay-schemas.ts into focused sub-modules
[relay-mesh-quality-improvements] [P3] Split relay-core.ts into focused sub-modules
tasks.description:
Split `packages/relay/src/relay-core.ts` (827 lines) into 3 focused sub-modules plus a facade using the facade pattern. All existing imports via `@dorkos/relay` barrel remain stable.

## Split Plan

| New File | Extracted Responsibility | Approx Lines |
|---|---|---|
| `relay-publish.ts` | `publish()` method logic, `PublishResult` type, rate limiting + circuit breaker integration | ~200 |
| `relay-subscriptions.ts` | `subscribe()` method logic, `SubscriptionRegistry` delegation, signal handler registration | ~150 |
| `relay-endpoint-management.ts` | `registerEndpoint()`/`unregisterEndpoint()`, Maildir store management, SQLite index updates | ~200 |
| `relay-core.ts` (facade) | Class skeleton, constructor, `init()`, `close()`, dependency composition, re-exports | ~200 |

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Analyze relay-core.ts structure

Read the entire file and identify:
- The `RelayCore` class methods and their boundaries
- Private helper methods used by specific public methods
- Shared state that multiple methods access
- The `PublishResult` type definition

### Step 2: Extract relay-publish.ts

Create `packages/relay/src/relay-publish.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Publish logic for the Relay message bus.
 *
 * Handles message publishing including subject validation, access control,
 * budget enforcement, rate limiting, and circuit breaker integration.
 *
 * @module relay/relay-publish
 */

// Extract:
// - PublishResult type (if defined in relay-core.ts)
// - The publish() logic as a standalone function or class method
// - Rate limiting and circuit breaker checks during publish
// - Any private helpers only used by publish
```

The extracted function should accept the necessary dependencies (EndpointRegistry, MaildirStore, SqliteIndex, etc.) as parameters or via a context object.

### Step 3: Extract relay-subscriptions.ts

Create `packages/relay/src/relay-subscriptions.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Subscription management for the Relay message bus.
 *
 * Handles subscribe/unsubscribe operations, SubscriptionRegistry delegation,
 * and signal handler registration.
 *
 * @module relay/relay-subscriptions
 */

// Extract:
// - subscribe() method logic
// - Signal handler registration
// - Any private helpers only used by subscribe
```

### Step 4: Extract relay-endpoint-management.ts

Create `packages/relay/src/relay-endpoint-management.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Endpoint lifecycle management for the Relay message bus.
 *
 * Handles endpoint registration/unregistration, Maildir store
 * directory management, and SQLite index updates.
 *
 * @module relay/relay-endpoint-management
 */

// Extract:
// - registerEndpoint() logic
// - unregisterEndpoint() logic
// - Maildir store management helpers
// - SQLite index update helpers
```

### Step 5: Rewrite relay-core.ts as facade

The facade retains the `RelayCore` class shell but delegates to extracted modules:

```typescript
/**
 * Main entry point for the Relay message bus.
 *
 * Thin facade composing publish, subscription, and endpoint management
 * sub-modules into a single cohesive API surface.
 *
 * @module relay/relay-core
 */
import { /* extracted publish function/class */ } from './relay-publish.js';
import { /* extracted subscribe function/class */ } from './relay-subscriptions.js';
import { /* extracted endpoint functions */ } from './relay-endpoint-management.js';

// Re-export public types from sub-modules
export { type PublishResult } from './relay-publish.js';

export class RelayCore {
  // Constructor, init(), close() stay here
  // Public methods delegate to extracted modules
}
```

### Step 6: Update index.ts exports

Check `packages/relay/src/index.ts` — the existing `export { RelayCore } from './relay-core.js'` and `export type { PublishResult } from './relay-core.js'` should continue to work since the facade re-exports them. If any types were moved to sub-modules, add re-exports from the facade.

### Step 7: Build and test

Run the full test suite for the relay package:
```bash
pnpm vitest run packages/relay/
pnpm typecheck
pnpm build
```

## Guidelines

- Sub-module files export named functions/classes/types (no default exports)
- The facade imports from sub-modules and either delegates or re-exports
- All existing `import { X } from '@dorkos/relay'` continue to work unchanged
- Share state through the RelayCore class instance (pass `this` or specific properties to extracted functions)

## Files Changed
- **Created:** `packages/relay/src/relay-publish.ts`
- **Created:** `packages/relay/src/relay-subscriptions.ts`
- **Created:** `packages/relay/src/relay-endpoint-management.ts`
- **Modified:** `packages/relay/src/relay-core.ts` (rewritten as thin facade ~200 lines)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `relay-core.ts` is under 250 lines (facade only)
- [ ] Each sub-module file is under 250 lines
- [ ] All existing imports from `@dorkos/relay` continue to work without changes
- [ ] All existing tests in `packages/relay/src/__tests__/relay-core.test.ts` pass without modification
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] `pnpm build` succeeds
- [ ] No circular dependencies between sub-modules

tasks.subject: [relay-mesh-quality-improvements] [P3] Split telegram-adapter.ts into focused sub-modules
tasks.description:
Split `packages/relay/src/adapters/telegram-adapter.ts` (761 lines) into 3 focused sub-modules plus a facade. All existing imports via `@dorkos/relay` barrel remain stable.

## Split Plan

| New File | Extracted Responsibility | Approx Lines |
|---|---|---|
| `telegram-inbound.ts` | Message parsing helpers, `handleUpdate()` logic, payload normalization | ~200 |
| `telegram-outbound.ts` | `deliver()` implementation, message chunking (4096-char limit), typing signal handling | ~180 |
| `telegram-webhook.ts` | Webhook setup, HMAC verification, HTTP server lifecycle | ~150 |
| `telegram-adapter.ts` (facade) | Class, polling mode, start/stop lifecycle, re-exports | ~180 |

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Analyze telegram-adapter.ts structure

Read the entire file and identify:
- Inbound message handling (Telegram update parsing, normalization to relay envelope format)
- Outbound delivery (message chunking for Telegram's 4096-char limit, typing indicators)
- Webhook-related code (setup, verification, HTTP server)
- Main class lifecycle (start, stop, polling loop)
- The `TELEGRAM_MANIFEST` constant

### Step 2: Extract telegram-inbound.ts

Create `packages/relay/src/adapters/telegram-inbound.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Telegram inbound message handling.
 *
 * Parses Telegram Bot API updates into Relay-compatible payloads.
 * Handles text messages, photos, documents, and other message types.
 *
 * @module relay/adapters/telegram-inbound
 */

// Extract:
// - handleUpdate() logic
// - Message type detection and parsing
// - Payload normalization to RelayEnvelope format
// - User/chat ID extraction helpers
// - Any inbound-only type definitions
```

### Step 3: Extract telegram-outbound.ts

Create `packages/relay/src/adapters/telegram-outbound.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Telegram outbound message delivery.
 *
 * Handles deliver() implementation including message chunking for
 * Telegram's 4096-character limit and typing signal management.
 *
 * @module relay/adapters/telegram-outbound
 */

// Extract:
// - deliver() implementation
// - Message chunking logic (split at 4096 chars)
// - Typing indicator send/stop
// - Message formatting (Markdown/HTML)
```

### Step 4: Extract telegram-webhook.ts

Create `packages/relay/src/adapters/telegram-webhook.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Telegram webhook management.
 *
 * Handles webhook URL registration with the Telegram Bot API,
 * HMAC signature verification, and the HTTP server lifecycle
 * for receiving webhook callbacks.
 *
 * @module relay/adapters/telegram-webhook
 */

// Extract:
// - Webhook setup/teardown functions
// - HMAC verification logic
// - HTTP server creation and lifecycle
// - Webhook URL registration with Bot API
```

### Step 5: Rewrite telegram-adapter.ts as facade

The facade retains the `TelegramAdapter` class and `TELEGRAM_MANIFEST` but delegates to extracted modules:

```typescript
/**
 * Telegram Bot API adapter for the Relay message bus.
 *
 * Supports both polling and webhook modes. Delegates inbound parsing,
 * outbound delivery, and webhook management to focused sub-modules.
 *
 * @module relay/adapters/telegram-adapter
 */
import { /* inbound functions */ } from './telegram-inbound.js';
import { /* outbound functions */ } from './telegram-outbound.js';
import { /* webhook functions */ } from './telegram-webhook.js';

export const TELEGRAM_MANIFEST = { /* ... */ };

export class TelegramAdapter implements RelayAdapter {
  // Constructor, start(), stop(), status remain here
  // Delegates to extracted modules for heavy logic
}
```

### Step 6: Verify tests

Run:
```bash
pnpm vitest run packages/relay/src/__tests__/adapters/telegram-adapter.test.ts
pnpm typecheck
pnpm build
```

## Guidelines

- Sub-module files export named functions (no default exports)
- The `TelegramAdapter` class and `TELEGRAM_MANIFEST` remain in the facade
- All existing `import { TelegramAdapter, TELEGRAM_MANIFEST } from '@dorkos/relay'` continue to work
- Sub-modules receive grammY Bot instance and config as parameters (no class inheritance)

## Files Changed
- **Created:** `packages/relay/src/adapters/telegram-inbound.ts`
- **Created:** `packages/relay/src/adapters/telegram-outbound.ts`
- **Created:** `packages/relay/src/adapters/telegram-webhook.ts`
- **Modified:** `packages/relay/src/adapters/telegram-adapter.ts` (rewritten as thin facade ~180 lines)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `telegram-adapter.ts` is under 200 lines (facade only)
- [ ] Each sub-module file is under 250 lines
- [ ] All existing imports from `@dorkos/relay` continue to work unchanged
- [ ] All existing telegram adapter tests pass without modification
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] `pnpm build` succeeds
- [ ] No circular dependencies between sub-modules

tasks.subject: [relay-mesh-quality-improvements] [P3] Split claude-code-adapter.ts into focused sub-modules
tasks.description:
Split `packages/relay/src/adapters/claude-code-adapter.ts` (906 lines, the largest file) into 3 focused sub-modules plus a facade. All existing imports via `@dorkos/relay` barrel remain stable.

## Split Plan

| New File | Extracted Responsibility | Approx Lines |
|---|---|---|
| `claude-code-agent-handler.ts` | `handleAgentMessage()`, session ID resolution, trace span creation, response streaming | ~250 |
| `claude-code-pulse-handler.ts` | `handlePulseMessage()`, pulse payload parsing, job execution | ~200 |
| `claude-code-queue.ts` | Queue management per agent, semaphore enforcement, `processWithQueue()` | ~150 |
| `claude-code-adapter.ts` (facade) | Class, start/stop/deliver API, status tracking, re-exports | ~250 |

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Analyze claude-code-adapter.ts structure

Read the entire file and identify:
- Agent message handling (session creation, message delivery, response streaming)
- Pulse message handling (scheduled job parsing, execution)
- Queue management (per-agent queues, semaphore/concurrency limits)
- Main class lifecycle and status tracking
- The `CLAUDE_CODE_MANIFEST` constant
- All exported types (`ClaudeCodeAdapterConfig`, `ClaudeCodeAdapterDeps`, etc.)

### Step 2: Extract claude-code-agent-handler.ts

Create `packages/relay/src/adapters/claude-code-agent-handler.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Agent message handling for the Claude Code adapter.
 *
 * Handles routing inbound messages to Claude Agent SDK sessions,
 * session ID resolution via binding strategies, trace span creation,
 * and response streaming back to the relay.
 *
 * @module relay/adapters/claude-code-agent-handler
 */

// Extract:
// - handleAgentMessage() or equivalent
// - Session ID resolution logic (per-chat, per-user, stateless strategies)
// - Trace span creation for agent interactions
// - Response streaming logic
// - Types specific to agent handling
```

### Step 3: Extract claude-code-pulse-handler.ts

Create `packages/relay/src/adapters/claude-code-pulse-handler.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Pulse (scheduled job) handling for the Claude Code adapter.
 *
 * Parses pulse payloads and executes scheduled jobs via the
 * Claude Agent SDK. Integrates with the PulseStore for job
 * status tracking.
 *
 * @module relay/adapters/claude-code-pulse-handler
 */

// Extract:
// - handlePulseMessage() or equivalent
// - Pulse payload parsing and validation
// - Job execution via Agent SDK
// - PulseStore integration
```

### Step 4: Extract claude-code-queue.ts

Create `packages/relay/src/adapters/claude-code-queue.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Queue management for the Claude Code adapter.
 *
 * Manages per-agent message queues with semaphore-based concurrency
 * enforcement. Ensures agents process messages sequentially while
 * allowing parallel processing across different agents.
 *
 * @module relay/adapters/claude-code-queue
 */

// Extract:
// - Queue data structure per agent
// - processWithQueue() or equivalent
// - Semaphore/concurrency enforcement
// - Queue cleanup on adapter stop
```

### Step 5: Rewrite claude-code-adapter.ts as facade

The facade retains the `ClaudeCodeAdapter` class and `CLAUDE_CODE_MANIFEST` but delegates to extracted modules:

```typescript
/**
 * Claude Code adapter for the Relay message bus.
 *
 * Routes messages between the Relay bus and Claude Agent SDK sessions.
 * Delegates agent message handling, pulse execution, and queue management
 * to focused sub-modules.
 *
 * @module relay/adapters/claude-code-adapter
 */
import { /* agent handler */ } from './claude-code-agent-handler.js';
import { /* pulse handler */ } from './claude-code-pulse-handler.js';
import { /* queue manager */ } from './claude-code-queue.js';

export const CLAUDE_CODE_MANIFEST = { /* ... */ };

// Re-export all public types
export type { ClaudeCodeAdapterConfig, ClaudeCodeAdapterDeps, ... };

export class ClaudeCodeAdapter implements RelayAdapter {
  // Constructor, start(), stop(), deliver(), status tracking
  // Delegates to extracted modules for heavy logic
}
```

### Step 6: Verify tests

Run:
```bash
pnpm vitest run packages/relay/src/adapters/__tests__/claude-code-adapter.test.ts
pnpm vitest run packages/relay/src/adapters/__tests__/claude-code-adapter-correlation.test.ts
pnpm vitest run packages/relay/src/__tests__/relay-cca-roundtrip.test.ts
pnpm typecheck
pnpm build
```

## Guidelines

- Sub-module files export named functions/classes/types (no default exports)
- The `ClaudeCodeAdapter` class and `CLAUDE_CODE_MANIFEST` remain in the facade
- All existing imports continue to work: `import { ClaudeCodeAdapter, CLAUDE_CODE_MANIFEST } from '@dorkos/relay'`
- All type exports (`ClaudeCodeAdapterConfig`, `ClaudeCodeAdapterDeps`, `ClaudeCodeAgentRuntimeLike`, `AgentSessionStoreLike`, `TraceStoreLike`, `PulseStoreLike`) must be re-exported from the facade
- Sub-modules receive dependencies as parameters rather than accessing class state directly

## Files Changed
- **Created:** `packages/relay/src/adapters/claude-code-agent-handler.ts`
- **Created:** `packages/relay/src/adapters/claude-code-pulse-handler.ts`
- **Created:** `packages/relay/src/adapters/claude-code-queue.ts`
- **Modified:** `packages/relay/src/adapters/claude-code-adapter.ts` (rewritten as thin facade ~250 lines)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `claude-code-adapter.ts` is under 300 lines (facade only)
- [ ] Each sub-module file is under 300 lines
- [ ] All existing imports from `@dorkos/relay` continue to work unchanged
- [ ] All existing Claude Code adapter tests pass without modification (3 test files)
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] `pnpm build` succeeds
- [ ] No circular dependencies between sub-modules

tasks.subject: [relay-mesh-quality-improvements] [P3] Split mesh-core.ts into focused sub-modules
tasks.description:
Split `packages/mesh/src/mesh-core.ts` (776 lines) into 3 focused sub-modules plus a facade. All existing imports via `@dorkos/mesh` barrel remain stable. Note that `mesh-topology.ts` already exists as a separate file.

## Split Plan

| New File | Extracted Responsibility | Approx Lines |
|---|---|---|
| `mesh-discovery.ts` | `discover()` async generator, `register()` method, `upsertAutoImported()` logic | ~200 |
| `mesh-agent-management.ts` | list/get/update agent operations, status snapshots, agent inspection | ~200 |
| `mesh-denial.ts` | deny/undeny/isDenied delegation, denial list querying | ~100 |
| `mesh-core.ts` (facade) | Class skeleton, constructor, init, close, reconciler startup, re-exports | ~200 |

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Analyze mesh-core.ts structure

Read the entire file and identify:
- Discovery-related methods (scanning, registration, auto-import)
- Agent management methods (list, get, update, status snapshots)
- Denial list methods (deny, undeny, isDenied)
- Core lifecycle (constructor, init, close, reconciler)
- The `MeshOptions` type

### Step 2: Extract mesh-discovery.ts

Create `packages/mesh/src/mesh-discovery.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Agent discovery for the Mesh module.
 *
 * Handles scanning for agents using pluggable discovery strategies,
 * registering discovered agents, and auto-import logic.
 *
 * @module mesh/mesh-discovery
 */

// Extract:
// - discover() async generator or equivalent
// - register() method logic
// - upsertAutoImported() logic
// - Discovery-related helpers
// - scanPaths configuration
```

### Step 3: Extract mesh-agent-management.ts

Create `packages/mesh/src/mesh-agent-management.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Agent management operations for the Mesh module.
 *
 * Provides list/get/update operations on registered agents,
 * status snapshots, and agent inspection capabilities.
 *
 * @module mesh/mesh-agent-management
 */

// Extract:
// - listAgents() / getAgent() / updateAgent()
// - Status snapshot methods
// - Agent inspection logic
// - getProjectPath() helper
```

### Step 4: Extract mesh-denial.ts

Create `packages/mesh/src/mesh-denial.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Denial list management for the Mesh module.
 *
 * Wraps the DenialList with MeshCore-level operations for
 * denying, undenying, and checking agent denial status.
 *
 * @module mesh/mesh-denial
 */

// Extract:
// - deny() / undeny() / isDenied() delegation
// - Denial list querying helpers
```

### Step 5: Rewrite mesh-core.ts as facade

The facade retains the `MeshCore` class shell but delegates to extracted modules:

```typescript
/**
 * Main entry point for the Mesh agent discovery and registry module.
 *
 * Thin facade composing discovery, agent management, and denial
 * sub-modules into a single cohesive API surface.
 *
 * @module mesh/mesh-core
 */
import { /* discovery functions */ } from './mesh-discovery.js';
import { /* agent management functions */ } from './mesh-agent-management.js';
import { /* denial functions */ } from './mesh-denial.js';

export type MeshOptions = { /* ... */ };

export class MeshCore {
  // Constructor, init(), close(), reconciler startup
  // Public methods delegate to extracted modules
}
```

### Step 6: Update index.ts if needed

Check `packages/mesh/src/index.ts` — existing `export { MeshCore } from './mesh-core.js'` should continue working. Verify `MeshOptions` type is still exported.

### Step 7: Verify tests

Run:
```bash
pnpm vitest run packages/mesh/src/__tests__/mesh-core.test.ts
pnpm typecheck
pnpm build
```

## Guidelines

- Sub-module files export named functions (no default exports)
- The `MeshCore` class and `MeshOptions` type remain in the facade
- All existing `import { MeshCore } from '@dorkos/mesh'` continue to work
- `mesh-topology.ts` already exists separately — move any remaining topology code there if found in mesh-core.ts
- Sub-modules receive dependencies (AgentRegistry, DenialList, etc.) as parameters

## Files Changed
- **Created:** `packages/mesh/src/mesh-discovery.ts`
- **Created:** `packages/mesh/src/mesh-agent-management.ts`
- **Created:** `packages/mesh/src/mesh-denial.ts`
- **Modified:** `packages/mesh/src/mesh-core.ts` (rewritten as thin facade ~200 lines)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `mesh-core.ts` is under 250 lines (facade only)
- [ ] Each sub-module file is under 250 lines
- [ ] All existing imports from `@dorkos/mesh` continue to work unchanged
- [ ] All existing tests in `packages/mesh/src/__tests__/mesh-core.test.ts` pass without modification
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] `pnpm build` succeeds
- [ ] No circular dependencies between sub-modules
